evals.prompt: I want to build an AI content pipeline where a researcher finds info on a topic, a writer creates a blog post from that research, and a reviewer checks it for accuracy. The supervisor should route between them. Using GPT-4o-mini for research/review and GPT-4o for writing.
evals.expected_output: A multi-agent system with DSPy modules for each agent (researcher, writer, reviewer), a LangGraph StateGraph with supervisor routing, and conditional edges. Should show how to use different models per agent.

assertions.name: has_separate_agent_modules
assertions.description: Defines distinct DSPy modules or signatures for researcher, writer, and reviewer agents

assertions.name: uses_langgraph_stategraph
assertions.description: Uses LangGraph StateGraph for orchestration with nodes and edges

assertions.name: has_supervisor_routing
assertions.description: Includes a supervisor node with conditional edges that routes to the correct agent

assertions.name: has_shared_state
assertions.description: Defines a TypedDict for shared state that agents read from and write to

assertions.name: agents_report_back
assertions.description: Each agent node returns to the supervisor after completing its work

evals.prompt: We need a customer support system where L1 handles simple questions, L2 handles complex technical issues, and L3 handles escalations that need database access. L1 should try first and escalate if it can't answer. L2 can escalate to L3. Need human approval before L3 takes any database actions.
evals.expected_output: A tiered support system with DSPy modules for each level, LangGraph orchestration with conditional handoff based on complexity, and interrupt_before for L3 database actions. Should include escalation logic.

assertions.name: has_tiered_agents
assertions.description: Defines separate DSPy modules for L1, L2, and L3 support agents with different capabilities

assertions.name: has_escalation_logic
assertions.description: Includes conditional routing that escalates from L1 to L2 to L3 based on complexity or confidence

assertions.name: has_human_in_loop
assertions.description: Uses interrupt_before or checkpoint pattern for human approval before L3 database actions

assertions.name: l3_has_tools
assertions.description: L3 agent uses dspy.ReAct or similar with database tools

assertions.name: uses_langgraph
assertions.description: Uses LangGraph StateGraph for orchestration, not raw if/else in a DSPy module

evals.prompt: I'm building a research assistant that needs to search 4 different sources in parallel (web, internal docs, academic papers, competitor analysis), then merge the results into a single brief. Each source search is its own specialist agent. Speed matters — can't run them sequentially.
evals.expected_output: A parallel multi-agent system using LangGraph Send() to fan out to 4 specialist agents simultaneously, then merge results. Each agent is a DSPy module. Should use the fan-out/merge pattern, not sequential chaining.

assertions.name: uses_send_for_parallel
assertions.description: Uses LangGraph Send() pattern for parallel fan-out to worker agents, not sequential edges

assertions.name: has_four_specialists
assertions.description: Defines separate agents or worker invocations for web, docs, papers, and competitor sources

assertions.name: has_merge_step
assertions.description: Includes a merge/combine node that aggregates results from all parallel agents into a final output

assertions.name: agents_are_dspy_modules
assertions.description: Each specialist agent uses DSPy (Predict, ChainOfThought, or ReAct) for its reasoning

assertions.name: uses_annotated_list_for_results
assertions.description: Uses Annotated[list, operator.add] or similar pattern so parallel results accumulate in shared state
